observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to investigate and make the necessary adjusting entries to restore accurate cash balances in the Cash in Bank - LCCA accounts.
We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the prompt submission of the required Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS), along with supporting schedules to enable a timely review of the accuracy and validity of bank balances and reconciling items.

We recommended that the Management:
Ensure the proper granting, utilization, and liquidation of all cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and
Direct all Accountable Officers to immediately settle their respective Cash Advances through demand letters.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to provide complete documentation and adequate disclosure for the outstanding obligations certified or charged to Accounts Payable so that the correctness and validity of related liability accounts can be verified and established in the audit.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Review, analyze, and reconcile the details of the Other Payables account; and
Provide complete documentation and adequate disclosure for the outstanding obligations so that the correctness and validity of the account could be verified and established in the audit.

We recommended the Management to:
Prepare adjusting entries to reflect the correct amount of Intangible Asset and Government Equity; and
Prepare adjusting entries to reflect the amortization expenses for the past periods.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to review and reconcile collections and remittances to determine any unremitted balance or recording errors and take necessary steps, such as remitting the amount to GSIS or adjusting the accounts.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to review and reconcile collections and remittances to determine any unremitted balance or recording errors and take necessary steps, such as remitting the amount to Pag-IBIG or adjusting the accounts.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to identify the details of any unremitted premiums and promptly remit them, if applicable. Additionally, measures should be taken to ensure the accurate and timely remittance of PhilHealth contributions to prevent future unremitted balances.

We recommended the Management to:
Strictly implement the retention money provisions under Paragraphs 6.1 to 6.2 of Annex E of IRR of RA 9184; and
Require contractors to submit a bank guarantee, irrevocable standby letter of credit, or surety bond callable on demand as substitute if contractors will opt to withdraw the retention money upon full payment after completion.
We recommended the Management to assess projects that have been completed for a year and issue a Certificate of Final Acceptance after ensuring compliance with contract plans and specifications.

We respectfully recommended that Management:
Direct the Local Engineer to prepare and submit duly Approved List of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the year to the Supervising Auditor and Audit Team Leader (SA & ATL) at the beginning of the year pursuant to Item No. 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 for verification. The same shall be regularly updated during the year in case of additional or new PPAs is to be implemented in that year, otherwise related expenses will be disallowed in audit and legal and administrative proceedings may be commence as evidence may warrant under the circumstances;
Promptly comply with the posting requirement thru a standard Project Signboard as designed; and
Submit the duly signed Monthly Monitoring Report and/or a Statement of Work Accomplishments (SWA) based on the interval of verification (%) percentage provided for in the Table.

We recommended that Management require the concerned offices/departments to implement immediately the various PPAs funded by the NGAs, if still on-going and warranted. Otherwise, remit to the source agencies or to the Bureau of the Treasury, whichever is appropriate, the unexpended balance of the trust fund.

We recommended that the Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit lacking delivery receipts or require the payee to refund the excess payment;
Require the Solid Waste Action Officer to submit documentation for the barangay Materials Recovery Facilities (MRFs) and Solid Waste Management (SWM) programs and activities to the audit team.
Require the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to revisit and update its 10-year solid waste management plan and to ensure the development of an environmentally compliant sanitary landfill, align planned programs and projects with the National Solid Waste Management Framework; and
Strictly comply with the environmental laws outlined in Republic Act (RA) No. 9003.

We recommended that Management direct the Offices concerned to ensure the full implementation of all projects funded by 20% DF within the budget year for optimum utilization of the fund and in order to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU.

We recommended the Management to:
Instruct the GAD focal person / GFPs to monitor the implementation of the identified PPAs and maximize utilization of the GAD funds to accomplish the purposes/objectives for which the GAD PPAs were identified in order to deliver immediate and optimum benefits to target beneficiaries;
Submit the distribution lists or any proof of actual implementation to avoid suspensions and/or disallowance in audit; and
Direct GAD Focal Point and implementing offices to ensure that proper documentation is maintained and submitted to facilitate verification and safeguard the integrity of public funds.

We recommended that Management submit a valid written explanation for our review and evaluation why the aforesaid transactions should not be disallowed in audit and legal and/or administrative sanctions be commenced against persons liable.
We further recommended that Management strengthen its procurement planning process to ensure that all procurement activities of the Municipality are in accordance with the approved Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and fully compliant with the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act No. 9184.
Finally, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) be directed to exercise due diligence in evaluating and determining the appropriate procurement modality for each procurement activity and to strictly adhere to the requirements of the procurement law to avoid potential adverse findings in future audits.

We recommended that Management install adequate internal control measures to minimize, if not stop, the recurrent errors in the preparation of checks and to charge the erring employees for the costs of the cancelled checks.

We recommended that Management direct:
Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Financial Statements and LDRRMF Utilization Report to address the ₱2,869,839.34 discrepancy, and ensure supporting documents are complete and accurate;
Municipal Accountant to update the Notes to Financial Statements with current and consistent data for the reporting period; and
Municipal Accountant to implement standard turnover procedures to ensure proper handover of records when personnel assigned to the LDRRMF account are reassigned.

We recommended that Management direct:
The GSO to submit the signed distribution list to the Audit Team as soon as possible to ensure proper documentation and verification of the relief goods distributed; and
The Municipal Accountant to ensure that all documentary requirements, such as distribution lists, are complete and properly attached when processing liquidations of cash advances.

We recommended that Management adequately plan and prioritize GAD-related programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) by focusing on high-impact, cost-effective, and sustainable interventions that address emerging and continuing gender issues in line with the objectives of the Magna Carta of Women.

We recommended that Management require   
the:

Municipal Accountant to correct the      
set-up RPT/SET Receivables by debiting   
Real Property Tax Receivable/Special     
Education Tax Receivable amounting to    
₱2.02 million and ₱3.47 million,         
respectively and crediting Deferred Real 
Property Tax Income/Deferred Special     
Education Tax Income by the same amount; 
and record yearly RPT/SET Receivables    
based on a complete and duly updated     
certified list of taxpayers at the       
beginning of the year;

Municipal Treasurer to update the        
certified list of taxpayers as of        
December 31, 2019 for the remaining 15   
barangays and the Municipal Accountant to
update the RPT/SET Receivables based on  
such list; and

Municipal Treasurer to designate         
personnel that will handle/focus only    
with updating regularly the RPTARs/TIC   
and at the beginning of each year,       
furnish the Municipal Accountant of a    
complete and duly updated certified list 
showing the name of taxpayers and the    
amount due and collectible for the year  
in accordance with Sec. 20 of the NGAS   
Manual.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in Part
II of this Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:          

submit the lacking documents and ensure
that the required documents are complete
before disbursement of funds to avoid     
suspension in audit; 

comply with posting requirements set by
the GPPB and DBM; and

require the concerned department to      
update the Municipality’s website and    
make the necessary posting as part of   
their public disclosure requirement.
observations.reason:
On-going
compliance with
audit
recommendations.
Lacking documents
were requested and
collated for
submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: (a) strictly adhere with the documentary requirements provided by the above rules and regulation to prevent adverse audit findings; (b) submit the lacking documents and ensure completeness, accuracy, and propriety of documents supporting the claims before processing  payments to suppliers; (c) strictly comply with the posting requirements set by the GPPB as mandated by the government; and (d) direct the BAC to transact only with suppliers who are technically, financially and legally capable.

We recommended that management: (a) Discontinue the practice of granting cash advances to the Municipal Treasurer; (b) The Municipal Treasurer designate or appoint a disbursing officer to handle the disbursing function pursuant to Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 50 of GAAM, Volume III.

We recommended that Management:
direct the GSO to ensure that the inventory of original OR/CR of the Municipality’s vehicles be under her custody; and
ensure that all insurable assets and properties of the Municipality are adequately covered/insured.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
liquidate fund transfers once projects are fully implemented and/or refund the unutilized amount from completed projects and require the appropriate recording in the books of accounts; and
prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for the total balance of ₱2.45 million as of December 31, 2021, otherwise, provide adequate disclosure in the notes to financial statements.

We recommended that Management immediately submit the MOA or Trust Agreement for all programs and projects. Henceforth, ensure that the COA Audit Team is always furnished a copy of MOA or Trust Agreement for validation of transactions involving Trust Fund.

We recommended that the Management direct the Offices concerned to ensure the full implementation of all projects funded by 20% DF for optimum utilization of the fund to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU.
We also recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) closely monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different development projects in accordance with the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) for timely delivery of basic and value-added services and benefits to its constituents.
We recommended that the Management instruct the BAC and its Secretariat to require the winning bidders to receive without delay the NTP to ensure early and timely delivery of projects.

We recommended that Management:
require the different offices/end-users in the agency to submit their Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) incorporating therein all their procurement requirements for the year and have them duly evaluated and reviewed for consolidation into a comprehensive, workable and realistic Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and follow the procurement methods and activities as stated in said APP;
strictly comply with the provisions enumerated in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and GPPB Resolution No. 11-2020; and
whenever practicable, consider consolidation of purchase requests for commonly used supplies needed by the different offices and adopt bulk purchases to promote efficiency in the procurement process.

We recommended that Management: (a) require the Municipal Treasurer, HRMO and Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the General Provisions of the GAA and adopt control measures to ensure that the municipal employees’ net take home pay will not be lower than the required minimum amount set by law after deducting statutory contributions and other allowed/authorized deductions; and (b)direct concerned Municipal officials to stop approving the loan applications of employees with net take home pay below the minimum balance required by the GAA.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned Office to submit the Contract of Service/Job Order and related documents within the period provided by existing rules and regulations to facilitate timely auditorial review and examination. Inability to submit these documents with the prescribed period shall be a cause for suspension of the transaction in audit, without prejudice to the administrative liability of the erring public officers or employees who are responsible for such failure.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to conduct and intensify tax campaigns to maximize collections especially on the delinquent taxpayers and impose remedies provided for, and exercise the taxing authority as mandated under the Local Government Code.
We recommended the Management to direct the Accounting and Treasury Office to ensure timely and accurate remittance of PhilHealth contributions to prevent the recurrence of unremitted balances.

We recommended that Management: (a) instruct the GAD focal person / GFPs to monitor the implementation of the identified PPAs and maximize utilization of the GAD funds to accomplish the purposes/objectives  for which the GAD PPAs were identified  to deliver immediate and optimum benefits to target beneficiaries; (b) ensure that GAD Accomplishment Report are prepared based on the GPB approved by the DILG, and any changes made therein be properly communicated in writing to the oversight body and attach all pertinent documents relative thereto; (c) revisit internal policies and procedures on GAD and implement a well-defined monitoring and evaluation system to properly assess GAD PPAs responsiveness in relation to gender mainstreaming.

We recommended that Management: (a) prioritize establishment of sanitary landfill; and (b) reconstitute the MSWMB to conform to the membership requirements of the Act and to optimize the involvement of civil society and other sectors as envisioned in the law.

We recommended that Management require   
the:

coordination between the Municipal       
Accountant and the GSO towards the       
implementation of the perpetual inventory
method in recording of supplies and      
materials; and establishment of          
sufficient stock room for proper storage 
of items of inventories;

GSO-designate/Inventory Committee to     
prepare and submit a complete RPCI yearly
in order to determine the correctness of 
the recorded inventories in the books of 
accounts of the Municipality and to      
ensure that semestral count of Inventory 
items is promptly conducted and the      
corresponding report is submitted on time
to the Audit Team;

Municipal Accountant and GSO to maintain
SLC and SC, respectively, for each type  
of inventories and reconcile the         
accounting and property records          
periodically to bring these accounts into
agreement; and

Municipal Accountant to prepare an       
adjusting entry for the proper           
classification of goods for distribution 
to people affected by                    
calamities/disasters by debiting “Welfare
Goods for Distribution” account amounting
to ₱1.86 million and crediting “Food     
Supplies Inventory” account by the same  
amount, and ensure proper classification 
of accounts in succeeding procurement of 
inventories.
observations.reason:
ongoing
construction of
stockroom for the
proper storage of
items of
inventory.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require:  

the investigation of the discrepancy of  
the results of the count by the property   
and accounting records be given priority 
by the GSO and MA;

the GSO to label all properties/equipment
showing complete description for easy    
identification and monitoring;

the disposal of unserviceable properties 
and the corresponding cost or value be   
dropped from the books of accounts;

the Municipal Accountant to validate the
long outstanding balance of CIP account  
and provide necessary adjusting entries  
to reclassify the same to appropriate PPE
accounts;

The GSO and Municipal Accountant to      
maintain the pertinent reports and       
property records of Road Networks to be  
able to provide reliable basis on the     
amounts presented in the financial       
statements;

the Municipal Assessor and officials     
concerned to prioritize the transfer of  
certificates of title to avoid exposing   
the lands to possible third-party claims
and land disputes; and

the Municipal Accountant to identify     
possible items needed to be excluded from
the PPE account and make proper          
adjustments, if deemed necessary.
observations.reason:
Undergoing
investigation of
the discrepancy in
PPE

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:          

require the different offices/end-users  
in the agency to prepare and submit their
Project Procurement Management Plan      
(PPMP) incorporating therein all their   
procurement requirements for the year and
have them duly evaluated and reviewed for
consolidation into a comprehensive,      
workable and realistic APP which would   
reflect the details of the procurement    
activities that it seeks to undertake     
within the calendar year; and strictly   
follow the procurement methods and       
activities as stated in their APP; 

whenever practicable, consider           
consolidation of purchase requests for   
commonly used supplies needed by the   
different offices and adopt bulk         
purchases to promote efficiency in the  
procurement process; and

ensure that disbursements are properly   
attached with complete documentation and 
standard forms are followed and          
completely filled out.
observations.reason:
Supplies were
bought on
piecemeal basis
and were
immediately
distributed to
requisitioning
office / end users
due absence of
storage room.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management abide by  
the rule on competitive bidding in all   
government procurement to ensure         
transparency, competitiveness,           
efficiency, and public accountability in 
the procurement process, resort only to   
alternative methods of procurement if    
justified by the conditions as prescribed
under the procurement law and rules, and  
in all cases, secure the most            
advantageous price for the government.
observations.reason:
On-going
compliance with
audit
recommendations

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:          

include PPAs chargeable against the Trust
Liabilities-DRRM account in the annual    
MDRRMFIP of the current year pursuant to
NDRRMC-DILG-DBM JMC No. 2013-1 and the  
same be prepared in substantial adherence
to the format prescribed under Annex A of
COA Circular No. 2012-002, thereby make
this trust fund available for disbursement;

require strict adherence to section 3.2 of
COA Circular No. 2009-001 regarding the
submission of POs within five (5) days from
issuance thereof; and

notify the auditor of the deliveries of      
procured items within twenty-four (24)     
hours from acceptance in adherence to the
provision of COA Cir No. 95-006; and

carefully assess the programs/projects/
activities charged against the MDRRMF,     
refrain from charging expenditures that do
not concern with disaster risk management
activities and strictly adhere to the       
provisions of COA Circular No.  2012-002
and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC Nos. 2013-1 and
2014-1.
observations.reason:
Compliance with
audit
recommendations
are currently
underway. Activities
are being
judiciously
planned and
streamlined to
ensure that they
are in consonance
with applicable
law, rules and
regulations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:          

ensure that only programs and projects   
listed in the MDRRMFIP and incorporated
in the AIP and Appropriation Ordinance be
charged against the MDRRMF; 

promptly submit the requirements for     
relief from property accountability in    
case of damage to property allegedly    
caused by force majeure before proceeding
with any restoration activity or project; and

enforce the warranty obligation of the 
contractor in cases where the damage was
due to structural or construction defects.
observations.reason:
On going
compliance with
audit
recommendations.
Requirements for
relief were not
immediately
submitted to the
audit team.
Warranty
obligation of
contractor were
not enforced.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:          

fully implement all uncompleted projects
as of December 31, 2020; and

execute all projects within the time frame
programmed for their completion to       
effectively and timely extend to the      
constituents the benefits of the agency’s
development project.
observations.reason:
The delay was due
to budgetary
constraints, additional
costs needed to
cover other factors
which were not
initially
considered in
planning and cost
estimates of
projects.
Not all projects
were executed
within the time
frame programmed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:          

establish clear-cut guidelines for the   
grant of communication allowances to     
officials and employees;

stop the granting of excessive           
communication allowances and consider the
application of postpaid plans offered by   
the available network providers with high
connectivity in the local area for        
economical reasons and to foster equity
in the grant of allowances in relation to
actual communication needs of the LGU and
for ease in monitoring and control of     
mobile expenses; and

refrain from the practice of              
reimbursement and abide with the proper
manner of government disbursements as    
provided in Section 93 of PD 1445.

We recommended that Management:          

create an Inventory Committee who shall, 
on a full-time basis and within a         
prescribed period, conduct the physical  
count and prepare the Report on the      
Physical Count of PPE properly including
maintenance of property cards;

require the Accounting Office to maintain
an updated PPE Ledger Cards for control
purposes and see to it that these records
facilitate identification and reconciliation;

designate on a full-time basis at least    
one personnel each from the Property and
Accounting Offices to reconcile the       
Report on the Physical Count of PPE,     
Property Cards, Property Ledger Cards,   
and accounting records, taking into      
consideration the noted deficiencies in   
audit: a cut- off date as of December 31,
2018 may be started to facilitate current
reconciliation, then move back from       
earier years; 

direct the accounting office to investigate
for the details of the long outstanding
balance of CIP account, and provide       
necessary adjusting entries to reclassify
the same to the appropriate PPE account;

require the accounting office to adhere to
the prescribed accounting rules relative to
the asset recognition under Property,     
Plant and Equipment, including use and
maintenance of Construction in Progress
account during the construction period of
an asset; and

initiate administrative investigation and
charges against responsible officers for
continued neglect and failure to comply
or perform their respective duties
relative to property, plant and equipment
despite repeated reminders from this      
Office.
observations.reason:
Restated in Part
II of this year’s
audit report
Lack of manpower,
heavy workload and
absence of
relevant data to
support beginning
balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the
Municipal Accountant to ensure that      
transactions pertaining to the Due to BIR
account be traced to prior years’        
accounting entries to ascertain the cause
of the negative balances and effect the   
necessary adjustments.

We recommended that Management refrain
from charging expenditures that do not   
concern with disaster risk management    
activities and strictly adhere to the     
provisions of COA Circular No.  2012-002,
and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC Nos. 2013-1 and
2014-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid
Waste Management Board:

review and revise its Municipal Solid      
Waste Management Plan in accordance with
the requirements of the law and have the
same approved by the National Solid Waste
Management Commission; and

ensure the sustainability, viability,
effectiveness and relevance of the plan as
required under Section 16 of RA 9003.

We recommended that Management strictly
adhere with GAD policies to implement GAD
related activities, properly coordinate    
with all the personnel/offices involved  
and ensure proper monitoring of the GAD
PPAs, to be able to implement all GAD    
PPAs as planned/budgeted to achieve GAD’s
objectives and address gender issues.
observations.reason:
Due to the
pandemic
(Covid-19) some of
the PPA’s were not
implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:          

fully implement all uncompleted projects
as of December 31, 2019; and

execute all projects within the time frame
programmed for their completion to       
effectively and timely extend to the      
constituents the benefits of the agency’s
development project.

Restated in Part
II of this year’s
audit report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require   
the liquidation of fund transfers once    
projects are fully implemented and/or    
refund the unutilized amount from        
completed projects and require the       
appropriate recording in the books of    
accounts.
observations.reason:
Almost all
infrastructures
project were
liquidated and
undisbursed amount
were returned
except for the DOH
programs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to:

ensure that all collections shall be deposited intact to the authorized depository bank daily or on the next banking day; and

record cash transactions in his cashbooks and Treasury records accurately by recording collections and deposits in the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) on the day they were collected and/or received from the respective collectors and on the day they were actually deposited in the depository bank, respectively.
observations.reason:
Collections totaling           ₱883,442.78 as of December 31, 2019 were not deposited intact and promptly with the authorized government depository bank within the prescribed time.

The said amounts were also recorded as deposits both in the cashbook and accounting records as of December 31, 2019.

observations.managementAction: Additional cash advance will not be granted unless the previous cash advance is liquidated.
observations.reason: Suspension of salaries was not implemented due to minimal salary of concerned AO (below ₱5,000.00 Net pay).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

instruct the Inventory Committee to include all properties in physical count and submit an updated and properly accomplished RPCPPE with all the needed information;

direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated PPE ledger cards to support GL account balances; the Property Officer to keep corresponding property cards for each category of PPE for control purposes; and see to it that these records contain adequate details/description of the properties such as model, serial number, location, cost and other information necessary to facilitate identification; and

require both the Accounting Division and Property Section to exert all efforts to reconcile their records on a regular basis so that discrepancies thereon are immediately corrected.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this year’s audit report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require:

that only programs and projects listed in the LDRRMF Investment Plan and incorporated in the Annual Investment Plan and Appropriation Ordinance be utilized under the LDRRM Fund; and

the submission of duly approved Ordinance authorizing any change in the budget and/or indicating the various items of appropriations affected and the reason for the change in accordance with Section 321 of the Local Government Code.
observations.reason: Expenditures for MDRRMO were charged against LDRRMF as discussed in Observation No. 5 of Part II of this year’s audit report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

require strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 regarding the submission of Purchase Orders and contracts within five (5) days from execution thereof; and

notify the Auditor of the deliveries of procured items within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance in adherence to the provision of COA Cir No. 95-006.

We recommended that Management:

submit immediately the 290 disbursement vouchers for CY 2018 otherwise, said disbursements will be suspended in audit; and

require the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly verify the vouchers and payrolls being forwarded by the MTO to ensure that all vouchers and supporting papers as reported in the Reports of Checks Issued are submitted.
observations.reason: Disbursement Vouchers totaling ₱3,656,061.26 damaged by flood due to Typhoon Usman remained unsubmitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management ensure:

full implementation of the 20% development fund embodied in the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality in compliance with Paragraph 5.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1;

that only programs and projects listed in the Annual Investment Plan and Appropriation Ordinance be utilized under the 20% Development Fund; and

that funds are spent exclusively for the specific purpose or project for which they have been appropriated.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this year’s audit report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

fully utilize the funds granted by the national agencies to effectively and timely extend to the constituents the benefits of the agency’s projects; and

ensure that the Memorandum of Agreements are kept intact so as to properly monitor the agency’s compliance with its specific terms and duration of implementation.
observations.reason:
Grants for CY 2019 and prior years totaling ₱16,604,228.00 were not fully implemented as of December 31, 2019.

Concerned offices/departments do not maintain file copies of MOAs.
Documents/attachments for other lots are being completed/reconstructed since some of the documents were lost at CENRO, Iriga City.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

prepare the adjusting entries in the respective fund’s book as shown in the foregoing table;

trace prior years’ variances and record adjusting entries in the respective funds to reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts; and

prepare subsidiary ledgers to facilitate reconciliation of these accounts.
observations.reason:
Adjusting entries for prior years were not yet made due to on-going reconciliation.

Reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts for CY 2016 and prior periods is still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:

     a) Municipal Accountant to record RPT/SET Receivable based on  a  duly
        certified    list of taxpayers; and


     b) Municipal Treasurer to designate personnel who  shall  handle/focus
        only  with  updating  the  Real  Property  Tax  Account   Registers
        (RPTARs)/Taxpayer’s Index Card (TIC) and at the beginning  of  each
        year, furnish the Municipal Accountant of  a  duly  certified  list
        showing the name of taxpayers and the amount  due  and  collectible
        for the year in accordance with Sec. 20 of the NGAS Manual.

We recommended that Management require the:  (a)  Inventory  Committee
to  prepare  and  submit  a  complete  Report  on  the  Physical  Count   of
Inventories as of December 31, 2019 and onward to determine the  correctness
of the recorded inventories; (b) MGSO to submit the Summary of Supplies  and
Materials Issued (SSMI) together with the corresponding  RIS  and  Inventory
Custodial Slips for the issued supplies/goods; (c) Municipal Accountant  and
MGSO to maintain supplies ledger cards and stock  cards,  respectively,  for
each type of inventories and reconcile the accounting and  property  records
periodically to bring  these  accounts  into  agreement;  (d)  maintain  the
prescribed records to ensure proper accounting and  promote  better  control
in the receipt and issuance of supplies; and (e) provide a  stock  room  for
proper storage of inventories.

We recommended that Management:


       a. create an Inventory Committee who shall, on a full-time basis  and
          within a prescribed period, conduct the physical count and prepare
          the Report  on  the  Physical  Count  of  PPE  properly  including
          maintenance of property cards;

       b. require the Accounting Office to maintain an  updated  PPE  Ledger
          Cards for control purposes  and  see  to  it  that  these  records
          facilitate identification and reconciliation;


       c. designate on a full time basis at least one  personnel  each  from
          the Property and Accounting Offices who shall reconcile the Report
          on the Physical Count of  PPE,  Property  Cards,  Property  Ledger
          Cards, and accounting records, taking into consideration the noted
          deficiencies in audit: a cut- off date as of December 31, 2018 may
          be started to facilitate current reconciliation,  then  move  back
          from earlier years;


       d. direct the accounting office  to  validate  the  long  outstanding
          balance of CIP account, and provide necessary adjusting entries to
          reclassify the same to the appropriate PPE accounts;


       e.  require  the  accounting  office  to  adhere  to  the  prescribed
          accounting rules relative to the asset recognition under Property,
          Plant and Equipment, including use and maintenance of Construction
          in Progress account during the construction period  of  an  asset;
          and


       f.  initiate  administrative  investigation   and   charges   against
          responsible officers for continued neglect and failure  to  comply
          or perform their respective duties relative to property, plant and
          equipment despite repeated reminders from this Office.

We recommended that Management direct the  Municipal  Accountant
to ensure that transactions pertaining to the Due to BIR account  be  traced
to prior years’ accounting entries to ascertain the cause  of  the  negative
balances and effect the necessary adjustments.

We recommended that Management refrain from charging expenditures that
do not concern with disaster risk management activities and strictly  adhere
to the provisions of COA Circular No.   2012-002,  and  NDRRMC-DBM-DILG  JMC
Nos. 2013-1 and 2014-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board:


       a.  review and revise the Municipal Solid Waste  Management  Plan  in
          accordance with the requirements of the  law  and  have  the  same
          approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission; and


       b. ensure the sustainability, viability, effectiveness and  relevance
          of the plan as required under Section 16 of RA 9003.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere with  GAD  policies  to
implement  GAD  related  activities,  properly  coordinate  with   all   the
personnel/offices involved and ensure proper monitoring of the GAD PPAs,  to
be able to implement all GAD  PPAs  as  planned/budgeted  to  achieve  GAD’s
objectives and address gender issues.

We recommend that Management: (a) require the BAC Secretariat to submit all contracts together with the supporting documents listed down in COA Circular No. 2009-001 to the Audit Team within the prescribed period; and (b) ensure that the attachments are complete and in order by providing a checklist of the documentary requirements for every contract submitted for review to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this year’s audit report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to:


 a. ensure that all collections  shall  be  deposited  intact  to  the
    authorized depository bank daily or on the next banking day; and


 b. record cash transactions in his  cashbooks  and  Treasury  records
    accurately by recording collections and deposits in the Report  of
    Collections and Deposits (RCD) on  the  day  they  were  collected
    and/or received from the respective collectors and on the day they
    were actually deposited in the depository bank, respectively.

We recommended that Management:


a. send demand letters to the  concerned  Accountable  Officers  (AOs)
   requesting immediate liquidation of the cash advance or  settlement
   of the amounts due from them;


b. suspend the salaries of erring accountable officers until such time
   that full settlement of their cash advances are effected;


c. immediately stop the grant  of  additional  cash  advances  to  all
   officers and employees with unliquidated cash advances  until  such
   time that previous cash advances are fully settled; and


d. ensure that rules and regulations on the grant and  liquidation  of
   cash advances are strictly observed.

We recommended that Management:

 a. instruct the Inventory Committee  to  include  all  properties  in
    physical count and submit an  updated  and  properly  accomplished
    RPCPPE with all the needed information;


 b. direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete  and  updated
    PPE ledger cards to support  GL  account  balances;  the  Property
    Officer to keep corresponding property cards for each category  of
    PPE for control purposes; and see to it that these records contain
    adequate details/description of  the  properties  such  as  model,
    serial number, location, cost and other information  necessary  to
    facilitate identification; and


 c. require both the Accounting Division and Property Section to exert
    all efforts to reconcile their records on a regular basis so  that
    discrepancies thereon are immediately corrected.

We recommended that Management require:


 a. that only programs and projects listed in  the  LDRRMF  Investment
    Plan  and  incorporated  in  the  Annual   Investment   Plan   and
    Appropriation Ordinance be utilized under the LDRRM Fund; and


 b. the submission of duly approved Ordinance authorizing  any  change
    in  the  budget   and/or   indicating   the   various   items   of
    appropriations  affected  and  the  reason  for  the   change   in
    accordance with Section 321 of the Local Government Code.

We recommended that Management:

a. require strict adherence to the provisions  of  COA  Circular  No.
   2009-001 regarding the submission of Purchase Orders and contracts
   within five (5) days from execution thereof; and
b. notify the Auditor of the  deliveries  of  procured  items  within
   twenty-four  (24)  hours  from  acceptance  in  adherence  to  the
   provision of COA Cir No. 95-006.

We recommended that Management: (a) submit immediately  the  290
disbursement vouchers for CY 2018  otherwise,  said  disbursements  will  be
suspended in audit; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant  to  thoroughly
verify the vouchers and payrolls being forwarded by the MTO to  ensure  that
all vouchers and supporting papers as reported  in  the  Reports  of  Checks
Issued are submitted.

We recommend that Management require the: a. MGSO and the Accounting Office to reconcile their respective records, investigate the differences noted in the two reports and adjustments, if any, be effected immediately; b. Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated PPE ledger cards to support General Ledger (GL) account balances and the General Services Officer to keep corresponding property cards for all PPE accounts; c. MGSO to file an application for disposal of unserviceable properties totaling ₱3,606,975.74 together with the duly accomplished IIRUP and appraised properties for validation and inspection of COA; and d. Inventory committee to conduct full inventory of all the properties and to update and properly accomplish the RPCPPE containing adequate details/description of the properties to facilitate its identification and determination of cost and indicate the physical condition of the property.
observations.reason: Restated in Part II of this year’s audit report

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: a. secure copies of the Torrens Titles of its lots drawn in the name of the Municipality from the Land Registration Authority to eliminate the risk that the municipality may be evicted from the lot they are presently occupying if ownership cannot be established; b. in coordination with concerned officials, prepare and maintain the complete list of lots owned by the municipality with details as to description, area and value; c. ensure proper safekeeping of the newly secured and subsequently acquired Land Titles for judicious endorsement to their successor; and d. recognize all the properties of the municipality in the books whether these are acquired through purchase, donation or any other mode of acquisition.
observations.reason: Documents/attachments for other lots are being completed/reconstructed since some of the documents were lost at CENRO, Iriga City.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) prepare the adjusting entries in the respective fund’s book as shown in the foregoing table; (b) trace prior years’ variances and record adjusting entries in the respective funds to reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts; and (c) prepare subsidiary ledgers to facilitate reconciliation of these accounts.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliatio n of the reciprocal accounts for CYs 2017 and 2018 were made.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts for CY2016 and priors periods is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the: (a) immediate submission of the 15 disbursement vouchers for CY 2017 to forestall the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance; (b) Municipal Accountant to thoroughly verify the vouchers and payrolls being forwarded by the MTO to ensure that all vouchers and supporting papers as reported in the Reports of Checks Issued are submitted; and (c) MTO to devise strategies so that the interval of the issuance of checks to the time they are actually claimed by the payees is shortened.
observations.reason: Ten (10) of the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers were transmitted to COA. An investigation is being conducted to locate the other five (5) vouchers per Accounting & Treasury records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly comply with the allocation of one per cent of IRA to support LCPC programs and activities of the LGU. We further recommend that Management direct the members of the LCPC to prepare and submit the reports required under DILG MC No. 2012-120 for the proper evaluation and monitoring of implementation of projects.

We recommend that Management: (a) fully implement all uncompleted projects as of December 31, 2017; (b) instruct the Offices of the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Budget and Municipal Planning and Development to coordinate closely in the monitoring of the implementation of the projects funded from the 20 per cent Economic Development Fund; (c) require the Municipal Engineer to maintain adequate records pertaining to the status of projects being implemented particularly the engineer’s logbook; and (d) execute all projects within the time frame programmed for their completion to effectively and timely extend to the constituents the benefits of the agency’s development projects.
observations.reason: Discussed in Part II of this year’s audit report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) require all JOs to register to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) under the classification of  non-VAT taxpayers engaged in business; (b) ensure that proper taxes are deducted from the JOs’ wages before such payments are made to them; (c) remit all taxes withheld on or before the tenth (10th) day of the month following the withholding period; (d) ensure that, prior to the hiring of the new JOs, their respective TINs are available and updated; and (e) coordinate with LGUs BIR Officer for any clarifications on the tax types and rates applicable to various government transactions.

We recommended that Management require the immediate submission of the lacking documents pertaining to the disbursements made in CY 2016. We further recommended that: a. the Municipal Accountant signs’ Box A of the B Disbursement Voucher only if all supporting documents are complete and proper, and attach a Checklist of all the required documents for every Disbursement Voucher to hasten the verification of the required supporting documents; the Municipal Treasurer signs Box B to certify as to availability of funds; and b. the Municipal Mayor signs Box C (Approved for Payment) only if all the documentations as enumerated on the Checklist are attached to the voucher.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to include all the accounts covering transactions of the Economic Enterprise-Loans special account in its separate financial statements in order that these financial statements would reflect the actual financial condition and results of its operations.

adopt the prescribed DTT, Monthly

direct the Municipal Accountant not to
process payments for fuel consumptions
in the absence of any of the duly filled-
out DTT, Monthly Report of Official
Travel and Monthly Report of Fuel
Consumption.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
(a) before procurement of construction
materials are made, execute
Memorandum of Agreement between the
Municipality and the Barangay so that
the terms and conditions on the
implementation of the infrastructure
projects and subsequent operations
thereof are clearly identified; (b) require
the Municipal Engincer to closely
supervise and monitor the
implementation of the projects and
conduct inspection after completion
thereof, and (¢) cnsure that the
Certificate of Completion as well as
photos  before, during and after
construction are attached to the
corresponding disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that Management; (a) ensure that all procurement of goods and services within a calendar year be cautiously and judiciously planned; (b) determine the estimated period and quantity within  which the particular goods or services will be needed by the agency; (c) require the BAC Secretariat to thoreughly review and consolidate the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) in the proposed APP; and (d) conduct public bidding if the consolidated cost is already more than the P 100,000.00 threshold set by the RIRR of RA No. 9184 to give equal opportunity to  other eligible - - bidders/suppliers.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to be cautious in the acceptance of any official receipts, sales invoices and other commercial invoices whenever payments to suppliers are made and to see to it that evidences of payments are compliant with BIR regulations.

We recommended that Management: (a) expedite the construction of the Materials Recovery Facility and strengthen the monitoring of the related ordinances for extensive compliance by the constituents and business establishments; (b) require that penalties for violations be imposed strictly; and (c) coordinate with the local police or Public Safety Office (PSO) in connection with the implementation of prohibition and penalizing the littering of garbage specifically in public places.

We recommended that Management
issue orders/appointments to
immediately institute 2 GAD Focal Point
System (GFPS) in accordance with the
provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
JMC  No. 2013-0l. We also
recommended that upon establishment of
the GFPS, orient the members of their
functions and responsibilities starting
from the GAD planning and budgeting
up to the impiementation and monitoring
thereof as well as the reports or
documents to be prepared and submitted
to the concerned agencies.

We recommended that Management: (a} fully implement all uncompleted projects as of December 31, 2015; (b) require the Municipal Engineer to inspect and monitor all the concreting projects where the payments of construction materials and equipment rentals were shouldered by the Municipality; and {c) execute all projects  within  the time frame programmed for their completion tc effectively and timely extend to the constituents the benefits of the agency’s development projects.
observations.reason:
Restated in this year’s
report
